Class SimFactory

java.lang.Object
sims.gameEngine.SimFactory
public class SimFactory extends Object
A utility factory class for creating and initializing Sims and their environments.

This class provides static methods to bootstrap the game world with default Sims, homes, skills, and needs. It ensures that each Sim is consistently initialized with a home, career, skill set, and needs, making it easier to start a new game or generate test scenarios.

Responsibilities:

  • defaultGame() – Creates a predefined list of Sims with careers, homes, skills, and needs.
  • defaultHome(String) – Builds a personalized home with default locations, activities, and upgrades.
  • createSim(String, int, int, Home, Career) – Constructs a fully initialized Sim with the given attributes.
  • createSkills() – Generates a baseline skill map for Sims, covering all primary need categories.
  • createNeeds() – Generates a baseline needs map for Sims, with decay rates for each need type.

By centralizing Sim creation logic, this class promotes consistency and reduces duplication across the game engine. It acts as the entry point for setting up new Sims and their environments.

  • Constructor Details

    • SimFactory

      public SimFactory()

  • Method Details

    • defaultGame

      public static List<Sim> defaultGame()
      Initializes a default game state with a predefined list of Sims.

      This method creates several Sim objects with preset attributes, including names, ages, homes, careers, skills, and needs. Each Sim is assigned a Career sector (IT, Finance, Healthcare) with a salary, and a default home created via defaultHome. Skills and needs are initialized using helper methods createSkills() and createNeeds().

      Default Sims Created:

      • Dave – IT sector, salary 5000
      • Tim – IT sector, salary 4000
      • Darius – IT sector, salary 4500
      • Rance – Healthcare sector, salary 4600
      • Reggie – Healthcare sector, salary 4300
      • Eliyaz – Finance sector, salary 4200
      • Thou Yong – Finance sector, salary 4700

      The created Sims are added to a List and returned, providing a ready-to-play starting point for the game.

      Returns:
      a list of default Sims with predefined attributes

    • defaultHome

      public static Home defaultHome(String name)
      Creates a default home for a Sim with predefined locations, activities, and upgrades.

      This method initializes a Home object with a set of standard HomeLocations (Bathroom, Living Room, Bedroom, Kitchen), each containing default activities. It also defines several HomeUpgrades that unlock additional activities when purchased.

      Default Activities:

      • Kitchen – Eat Sandwich (Hunger)
      • Living Room – Watch TV (Fun), Scroll Social Media (Social)
      • Bedroom – Sleep (Energy)
      • Bathroom – Shower (Hygiene), Use Toilet (Bladder)

      Available Upgrades:

      • Oven (Kitchen) – Unlocks Pizza activity
      • Coffee Machine (Kitchen) – Unlocks Coffee activity
      • Sofa (Bedroom) – Unlocks Nap activity
      • Cards (Living Room) – Unlocks Play Card Games activity

      After creating activities and upgrades, they are assigned to the appropriate home locations. Finally, all locations are added to the home, which is returned for assignment to a Sim.

      Parameters:
      name - the name of the Sim, used to personalize the home name
      Returns:
      a Home object with default locations, activities, and upgrades

    • createSim

      public static Sim createSim(String name, int simListSize, int age, Home home, Career career)
      Creates and initializes a new Sim with the specified attributes.

      This method constructs a Sim object with a given name, ID, and age. It then assigns the Sim a Home, sets their current location to the first available HomeLocation, and associates them with a Career. Additionally, the Sim is initialized with default needs and skills using helper methods createNeeds() and createSkills().

      Initialization Steps:

      • Create a new Sim with name, ID, and age.
      • Assign default needs via createNeeds().
      • Set the Sim’s home and current location (first location in the home).
      • Assign the specified career.
      • Initialize default skills via createSkills().
      Parameters:
      name - the Sim’s name
      simListSize - the Sim’s unique identifier or index in the Sim list
      age - the Sim’s age
      home - the Sim’s home environment
      career - the Sim’s career assignment
      Returns:
      a fully initialized Sim object

    • createSkills

      public static Map<String, SkillManager> createSkills()
      Creates and initializes a default skill map for a Sim.

      This method constructs a HashMap where each key represents a specific need category (e.g., Hunger, Hygiene, Energy, Bladder, Fun, Social), and each value is a new SkillManager instance responsible for tracking and managing the Sim's skill progression in that category.

      Default Skills Created:

      • Hunger
      • Hygiene
      • Energy
      • Bladder
      • Fun
      • Social

      The returned map provides a consistent baseline skill set for each Sim, ensuring that all primary needs are represented and can be managed throughout gameplay.

      Returns:
      a map of need categories to their corresponding SkillManager objects

    • createNeeds

      public static Map<String,need> createNeeds()
      Creates and initializes a default needs map for a Sim.

      This method constructs a HashMap where each key represents a specific need category (e.g., Hunger, Hygiene, Energy, Bladder, Fun, Social), and each value is a new need object initialized with a decay rate. The decay rate determines how quickly the need decreases over time in the simulation, requiring the Sim to perform activities to replenish it.

      Default Needs and Decay Rates:

      • Hunger – 0.2
      • Hygiene – 0.01
      • Energy – 0.3
      • Bladder – 0.02
      • Fun – 0.005
      • Social – 0.003

      The returned map provides a consistent baseline set of needs for each Sim, ensuring that all primary aspects of well-being are represented and can be managed throughout gameplay.

      Returns:
      a map of need categories to their corresponding need objects
